{{Quote|I'm Lord Adasca–welcome to my world. On behalf of all our corporate citizens, welcome to Adascopolis. Coruscant may be the heart of the Republic–but the mind that dreams is right here on Arkania.|A hologram of Arkoh Adasca played at the Adascopolis spaceport|<ref name="kotor16" />}}
'''Adascopolis''' was the ancient [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] and the ancestral seat of the [[House of Adasca]], a rich and powerful family that ran [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]], or Adascorp. Nestled within Arkania's [[Water/Legends|snow]]-covered [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], Adascopolis had strict segregationist policies in place by around [[3963 BBY]]—members of the [[Bioengineering/Legends|genetically-engineered]] [[Subspecies/Legends|sub-species]] known as the [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoots]] were forced to live outside the [[City/Legends|city]] in derelict [[Mining/Legends|mining]] camps, apart from [[Blood/Legends|pureblood]] [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanians]]. 

During the [[Mandalorian Wars]], Adascorp was run by [[Arkoh Adasca]], the scion of the House of Adasca, who opened the [[Adascopolis Financial Exchange]] in the city. Around 3963 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], an Arkanian Offshoot named [[Jarael]] disguised herself as a pureblood Arkanian and sneaked into Adascopolis, hoping to get a medical diagnosis for her ailing friend, an elderly Offshoot named [[Gorman Vandrayk|Camper]]. However, Camper's [[Gene/Legends|genetic]] profile was recognized as that of Gorman Vandrayk, a former Adascorp research [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] whom Arkoh Adasca required for his future plans.

==Description==
{{Quote|Is it always so cold here?|Jarael|<ref name="kotor16" />}}
[[File:Arkania_space.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Adascopolis was located on the planet Arkania]]
Adascopolis was a [[City/Legends|city]] on<ref name="kotor16" /> the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]<ref name="adascorp" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]],<ref name="kotor16" /> and was the traditional seat of Arkania's venerated [[House of Adasca]].<ref name="cswe" /> Located amongst the world's freezing tundra and [[Water/Legends|snow]]-covered [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]],<ref name="kotor16" /> Adascopolis was both the ancient [[Capital/Legends|capital]]<ref name="cswe" /> and a major corporate and financial center for Arkania. Adascopolis mainly consisted of towers and tall buildings poking out of the snow-covered ground, along with [[Mining/Legends|mining]] camps that doubled as [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] settlements on the city's outskirts. Although Offshoots were not allowed into Adascopolis by about [[3963 BBY]], with the right disguise, it was possible for Offshoots to sneak into the city. Adascopolis had an extremely cold and windy climate to go with its snow-covered terrain.<ref name="kotor16" />

==History==
{{Quote|My friends and I have been sneaking into Adascopolis for years. You just need the right look.|Zadawi, to Jarael|<ref name="kotor16" />}}
Adascopolis was the ancient capital of the planet Arkania and the seat of Arkania's House of Adasca,<ref name="cswe" /> a rich and venerated family that ran a biomechanical corporation commonly known as [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]]. The Arkanians were keen [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineers]], and they eventually created the Arkanian Offshoot [[Subspecies/Legends|sub-species]] to aid in mining operations, including digging for valuable [[Gemstone/Legends|gems]] just outside of Adascopolis. At some point before around 3963 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], the mine ran dry—not allowed to enter Adascopolis, the Offshoots remained in their ramshackle settlement and lived in relative squalor. With the outbreak of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Battle of Serroco|devastation]] of the planet [[Serroco]] around 3963 BBY, there was a certain breakdown of civility in Adascopolis that was noticeable to visitors.<ref name="kotor16" /> Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Adascorp opened the [[Adascopolis Financial Exchange]] in the city, an effort that Adascorp hoped would move [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] center of commercial gravity toward Arkania, which was advertised in [[Adascorp Fiscal Period Financial Report and Outlook: Message from the Chief Executive (report)|''Adascorp Fiscal Period Financial Report and Outlook: Message from the Chief Executive'']], an Adascorp news report.<ref name="adascorp" /> Around 3963 BBY, an Arkanian Offshoot named [[Jarael]] traveled to Adascopolis looking to obtain a medical diagnosis for her friend, an elderly Offshoot known as [[Gorman Vandrayk|Camper]], who was seriously ill.<ref name="kotor16" />

[[File:Jarael_New_Headshot.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Jarael (pictured) snuck into Adascopolis.]]
Unfamiliar with Adascopolis' segregationist policies, Jarael was spotted at the Adascopolis [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and forced to leave the city. With the help of a young Offshoot named [[Zadawi]], Jarael was able to don a convincing [[Blood/Legends|pureblood]] Arkanian disguise and gain entrance to Adascopolis. Jarael bribed a [[Unidentified Arkanian doctor|doctor]] into helping her,<ref name="kotor16" /> but when Adascorp analyzed his sample and recognized Camper's [[Gene/Legends|genetic]] profile—"Camper" was actually a former Adascorp research [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] named Gorman Vandrayk, whom Adascorp head [[Arkoh Adasca]] needed to carry out future plans—Adasca ordered his men to take the Offshoot into custody, knowing that he needed Jarael in order to get to Vandrayk.<ref name="kotor17">[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]]</ref> After a violent skirmish in the medical center, they succeeded,<ref name="kotor16" /> bringing Jarael up to Adasca's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]''.<ref name="kotor17" />

Adasca's plan was to use his control of the [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]]—massive [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] creatures who devoured stellar matter at an incredible rate—as a bargaining chip to make himself and Adascorp into a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power. However, when Adasca attempted to sell the exogorths to the highest bidder, his plot was foiled, and he was [[First Battle of Omonoth|killed]].<ref name="kotor21">[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]]</ref> The whereabouts of the only other remaining heir to the House of Adasca had been unknown since the devastation of Serroco, leaving the door open for a group of investors, headed by the [[Draay Trust]], to file a legal challenge in Adascopolis for the right to bid for control of Adascorp. This filing and the city itself were mentioned in ''[[The Admiral's List: Karath Home Safely]]'', an issue of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] official communique ''[[The Admiral's List]]''.<ref name="karath">{{KOTORbackups|22}}</ref>

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|It's like we've been put down so long that now we're a party to our own degradation.|Zadawi|<ref name="kotor16" />}}
[[File:Zadawi.jpg|thumb|Arkanian Offshoots like Zadawi (pictured) had to disguise themselves as pureblood Arkanians to sneak into Adascopolis.]]
Adascopolis was the traditional seat of the House of Adasca, and by around 3963 BBY, it was mostly inhabited by pureblood Arkanians. Arkanian Offshoots were not allowed in the city, instead living in the derelict mining camps on the outskirts of town. Offshoots were allowed at the city spaceport but were not permitted to enter the city proper—some older Offshoots justified the policy as being for their own health since most pureblood illnesses and diseases were extremely deadly to Offshoots. Some Offshoots, including Zadawi and her friends, disguised themselves as pureblood Arkanians from time to time so they could sneak into the city.<ref name="kotor16" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|While you're here, be sure to visit our bioengineering facilities–the most advanced in the galaxy. And our materials processing labs are at the forefront of the Republic's drive for victory against the Mandalorian invaders.|A hologram of Arkoh Adasca|<ref name="kotor16" />}}
Visitors to Adascopolis landed at the Adascopolis spaceport, where they were greeted by a massive [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of Arkoh Adasca—from there, [[shuttlebus]]es took travelers either to the city or, if they were Offshoots, to their settlements on the edge of Adascopolis. The spaceport had a [[Stun baton/Legends|stun baton]]-wielding security force that ensured Offshoots did not attempt to enter Adascopolis. Adascopolis also boasted the most advanced bioengineering facilities in the galaxy as of around 3963 BBY, materials processing labs that aided the Republic during the Mandalorian Wars,<ref name="kotor16" /> and the Adascopolis Financial Exchange.<ref name="adascorp" /> Adascopolis also contained a state-of-the-art [[Adascopolis medcenter|medical facility]] in a tower, although while the [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] there balked at treating Offshoots, they could be convinced to for the right price.<ref name="kotor16" />

==Behind the scenes==
Adascopolis first appeared in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 16|sixteenth issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']]. The issue was written by [[John Jackson Miller]], illustrated by [[Brian Ching]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="kotor16" /> on [[May 2]], [[2007]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/14-397/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-16|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #16}}</ref>
